issue_title: Bug: `SensitiveTextRedactor` mangles `Authorization: Bearer` headers and its greedy token regex eats surrounding structural characters (`}`, `)`) along with the secret
issue_body: ### What happened? [`SensitiveTextRedactor.redact(text: String)`](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/security/SensitiveTextRedactor.kt#L15-L27) contains **two distinct bugs** that fire together whenever an `Authorization: Bearer ` substring is redacted as part of a larger log line — which is the exact case for tool-execution log strings written by `executeAndRender` and surfaced on the Tools tab and in `Export Debug Trace`. ### Bug A — `Authorization` header structure is destroyed The second assignment pattern uses `(?i)(authorization:\s*bearer\s+)[^\s,;]+` and shares the same replacement lambda as the first pattern: ```kotlin redacted = pattern.replace(redacted) { match -> val prefix = match.value.substringBeforeAny(listOf(":", "=")) "$prefix=[REDACTED]" } ``` For input `Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz`: - `match.value` = `Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz` - `substringBeforeAny(listOf(":", "="))` returns `Authorization` (split at the first `:`) - replacement = `Authorization=[REDACTED]` The output is `Authorization=[REDACTED]`. The `Bearer` keyword is dropped, and the HTTP-header `:` is rewritten to an assignment `=`. The first capture group (`(authorization:\s*bearer\s+)`) is captured but never used — the regex is written as if a `"$1[REDACTED]"`-style replacement were intended, and that is presumably the original intent. ### Bug B — the greedy `[^\s,;]+` token consumes trailing `}`, `)`, `]`, `>` etc. `[^\s,;]+` is greedy and only excludes whitespace, comma, and semicolon. Closing brackets, parentheses, angle brackets, quotes, and any other structural character get eaten as part of the "token" and replaced along with the secret. Concretely, the tool-execution log string produced by `MainActivity.executeAndRender` for a `type_text` call with `text=Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz` is: ``` type_text({text=Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz}) -> false: typeIntoFocusedField ``` After redaction, the actual output is: ``` type_text({text=Authorization=[REDACTED] -> false: typeIntoFocusedField ``` The `})` that closes the args dict and the tool-call parenthesis is **gone** — the regex matched `Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz})` (eating the closing `})`) and replaced the whole match with `Authorization=[REDACTED]`, throwing the brackets away. Same class of structural damage happens with: - `password=secret123}` inside an args dict — `password=[REDACTED]`, closing `}` deleted - `api_key=sk-abc.def\nsomething` — the regex would also eat through anything not whitespace/comma/semicolon, although newlines do happen to count as whitespace so this particular case stops at end-of-line - any JSON-ish args printout where the secret is the last value before `}` or `)` The mangling is visible in the Tools tab's "Latest result" card after the [bug2 fix](touchpilot/bug2.md) (or in the unified card on `main`), in approval cards rendered by `buildApprovalMessage`, and in trace files written by `exportDebugTrace`. The audit-log section on the Logs tab is **not** affected (it records the *un*redacted tool name and a separately-formatted args string via `ToolExecutionLog`), which makes the bug particularly confusing: a user comparing the "Latest result" card to the audit log below it sees mismatched structure for the same action. ### Expected behavior - The `authorization: bearer` pattern should preserve its capture group, producing `Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]` (or whatever case the original used). - The token-matching portion of every assignment pattern should not consume balanced/closing structural characters such as `}`, `)`, `]`, `>`, `"`, `'`. The safest fix is to narrow the character class — for example replace `[^\s,;]+` with `[^\s,;\}\)\]\>"']+` or, more robustly, capture the secret in its own non-greedy group and use `"\$1[REDACTED]"` as the replacement so the closing characters fall outside the match entirely. - The two assignment patterns should use replacement strings tailored to their capture groups (or be expressed as typed `RedactionRule` objects with per-rule replacement logic), so a future rule does not silently inherit a wrong replacement template. After the fix, the example log line should become: ``` type_text({text=Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]}) -> false: typeIntoFocusedField ``` — closing `})` intact, header structure intact, secret redacted. ### Steps to reproduce ### Fastest repro: a JVM unit test (no emulator required) 1. From the touchpilot project root, add a file `app/src/test/java/dev/touchpilot/app/security/SensitiveTextRedactorAuthHeaderRepro.kt`: ```kotlin package dev.touchpilot.app.security import kotlin.test.Test import kotlin.test.assertEquals class SensitiveTextRedactorAuthHeaderRepro { @Test fun authorizationBearerHeaderIsMangled() { val input = "Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz" assertEquals("Authorization=[REDACTED]", SensitiveTextRedactor.redact(input)) } @Test fun greedyRegexEatsTrailingStructuralCharacters() { val input = "type_text({text=Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz}) -> false: typeIntoFocusedField" assertEquals( "type_text({text=Authorization=[REDACTED] -> false: typeIntoFocusedField", SensitiveTextRedactor.redact(input) ) } } ``` 2. Run it: ``` .\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest --tests dev.touchpilot.app.security.SensitiveTextRedactorAuthHeaderRepro ``` 3. Observe both tests **pass on `main`** — they assert the broken output, so passing tests prove the bugs exist. After the fix, both assertions should be updated to expect `Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]` and the structurally-intact log line, and the test will pin the regression. ### End-to-end repro on a device or emulator 1. Build, install, and launch (accessibility service does not need to be enabled — the bug fires regardless because `executeAndRender` redacts the log string even when the underlying tool fails): ``` .\gradlew.bat assembleDebug adb install -r app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk adb shell am start -n dev.touchpilot.app/.MainActivity ``` 2. Tap the **Tools** tab. 3. Tap the input labelled **Text to type into focused field** and enter: ``` Authorization: Bearer abc123xyz ``` 4. Tap **Type Into Focused Field**. 5. Scroll the Tools tab to the **Latest result** card at the bottom. Observe the displayed string is: ``` type_text({text=Authorization=[REDACTED] -> false: typeIntoFocusedField ``` — the closing `})` is missing and the `Bearer` keyword has been dropped. 6. (Optional, for the trace path) Tap the **Logs** tab → **Export Debug Trace**, then `adb pull` the resulting file from `/sdcard/Android/data/dev.touchpilot.app/files/debug-traces/`. The file's recorded "Current screen" section runs the same redactor and will exhibit the same structural damage if the captured screen contains an `Authorization` header or any matching `key=value` field whose value is followed by a closing bracket. ### Device or emulator LDPlayer 9 on Windows 10 Pro x64. ### Android version Android 9 Pie, API level 28 ### TouchPilot commit _No response_ ### Accessibility service enabled? Yes ### Areas involved - [ ] Android control - [ ] Agent loop - [ ] Local router / local inference - [ ] Skills - [ ] MCP - [ ] Security / approvals - [x] UI ### Logs, screenshots, or traces
